obligation: Default backends must not report fixed-buffer capability, and fixed I/O must fall back to ordinary block I/O.
Caller keeps registered buffer pointers valid and unmoved until explicit unregister or backend drop.

AlignedBlock storage remains owned by BufferManager; raw traversal references stay read-leased while cached, mutable page access requires an exclusive write lease, and dirty flushes fail closed while a mutable lease is active.
evidence: BufferPageLease.tla; buffer_manager lease/flush tests; traversal_context pin tests; storage correspondence

obligation: BufferManager may use fixed I/O only after registration succeeds and the backend reports fixed-buffer support.

evidence: buffer_manager fixed-buffer registration unit test; IoUringFixedBufferOwnership.tla
